title: Update README and installation docs for the new install/packaging story
action: Update README.md's Configuration table so the static_dir row describes it as an optional override for a locally-built UI (embedded assets are the default, no toolchain required to run a prebuilt binary). Update README.md's Install section and docs/installation.md's prerequisites/Option C to explain install.sh now tries a prebuilt-binary download first (no Rust/Node toolchain needed when a matching release exists, since embed-pkg-3 relocated the cargo/npm prereq check to only gate the source-build fallback) and only needs the compile toolchain when it falls back to building from source — keep the git-clone-first step everywhere (per approach.md, the invocation model is unchanged, only how the binary is obtained). Also update docs/installation.md's step-3 sample doctor output (~line 68, currently `✓ web UI     static`) and the troubleshooting table's `✗ web UI: no built UI` row (~line 104) to match embed-pkg-2's new doctor.rs behavior — the web-UI check no longer has a hard-failure mode once embedding is compile-time-guaranteed, so both surfaces need re-wording to reflect what the check now actually reports (read the real doctor.rs output after embed-pkg-2 caps, don't guess the wording). Check docs/deployment.md for any install.sh/static_dir assumptions that need the same correction (it currently only references install.sh once with no static_dir content — confirm no change is needed there, or make the minimal one if discovery finds otherwise). Do not invent new install methods beyond what embed-pkg-3 actually implemented.

truths: README.md and docs/installation.md accurately describe the download-first, build-fallback install.sh behavior implemented in embed-pkg-3
docs/installation.md's sample doctor output and troubleshooting table match embed-pkg-2's actual doctor.rs wording, not stale pre-embedding text
No doc claims a standalone download-only install with no git checkout (out of scope, per approach.md)

artifacts.substantive: Configuration table static_dir row and Install section updated

prerequisites, Option C, sample doctor output, and troubleshooting table all updated to reflect the prebuilt-download path and the new doctor.rs wording; mentions 'prebuilt' so the verify command has something real to assert

prohibitions: No changes to docs/usage.md content (it has no install-path content to update, confirmed during planning)

trace.outcome: Updated README.md, docs/installation.md, and docs/deployment.md to describe install.sh's prebuilt-download-first/source-build-fallback behavior and doctor.rs's always-succeeding web UI check (embedded-by-default, no failure mode).
trace.verify_output: exit 0; docs/installation.md contains 'prebuilt' 2 times, README.md contains it at least once
